symptom: a vague upper belly discomfort and a bit of bloating that came and went for weeks
hook: a loop of bowel had slipped up between the liver and the diaphragm where nothing is meant to sit
whatYouSee: A pocket of bowel gas is sitting up between the liver and the right side of the diaphragm where only solid liver should be.
whyItMatters: It is usually harmless but it can be mistaken for dangerous free air under the diaphragm which sends people to surgery they never needed.
treatment: When it causes no trouble it is left alone. Only the rare loop that twists or blocks needs an operation.
takeaway: Sometimes the scariest looking scan is the one that needs nothing at all.
igOptions: A. A hole in the bowel
B. Bowel trapped above the liver

laypersonQuestion: Does something look out of place up top?
seedHint: Look between the liver and the dome above it and ask yourself if that belongs there.

generated.threadsCaption:
A patient came in with a vague upper belly discomfort and a bit of bloating that came and went for weeks.

Then the X-ray loaded 😭

And a loop of bowel had slipped up between the liver and the diaphragm where nothing is meant to sit.

Quick diagnosis challenge 🩻

What's the most likely diagnosis?

Wild guesses are welcome 👀
generated.threadsAnswer:
Answer: Chilaiditi syndrome

👀 What you see:
A pocket of bowel gas is sitting up between the liver and the right side of the diaphragm where only solid liver should be.

🦴 Why it matters:
It is usually harmless but it can be mistaken for dangerous free air under the diaphragm which sends people to surgery they never needed.

💊 Treatment:
When it causes no trouble it is left alone. Only the rare loop that twists or blocks needs an operation.
